title: Adaptive Prototype-Aware Consistency with Learnable Augmentation Policies for Single-Image Test-Time Adaptation
description: A test-time adaptation method that introduces a meta-learned augmentation policy network to select augmentations based on prototype similarity, combined with confidence-weighted consistency to source prototypes. Unlike prior work using fixed augmentations (MEMO, DPE) or entropy minimization (TENT, MEMO), our approach learns corruption-specific augmentation strategies and adaptively weights prototype guidance based on consistency scores.
motivation: Existing TTA methods suffer from three limitations: (1) Confirmation bias in entropy-based methods (TENT, MEMO), (2) Fixed augmentation strategies that ignore test image characteristics, and (3) Equal weighting of prototypes regardless of reliability. Recent prototype-based methods (DPE for VLMs, CPA for class-wise alignment, DPL for contrastive learning) have shown promise but do not address augmentation selection. We fill this gap by introducing learnable, prototype-aware augmentation policies and confidence-weighted prototype guidance for pure vision models.
proposed_approach: 1) Pre-compute class prototypes from source training data and train a lightweight Meta-Augmentation Policy Network (Meta-APN, ~10K params) to predict augmentation parameters based on prototype similarity. The Meta-APN uses Gumbel-Softmax for differentiable discrete operation selection and REINFORCE as fallback. 2) Implement a Heuristic-APN baseline (distance-based severity selection) to rigorously justify the complexity of learned policies. 3) At test time, use Meta-APN to select corruption-specific augmentations for each test image. 4) Compute confidence scores for source prototypes based on their consistency across augmented views. 5) Adapt model parameters (BatchNorm) using confidence-weighted consistency loss between augmented view predictions and their weighted nearest prototypes. 6) Selectively adapt only when test features are within source distribution.

related_work.paper: TENT: Fully Test-Time Adaptation by Entropy Minimization
related_work.difference: TENT minimizes prediction entropy for batch adaptation but suffers from confirmation bias and requires batches. We use prototype-guided consistency with learned augmentations for single-image adaptation.

related_work.paper: MEMO: Test Time Robustness via Adaptation and Augmentation
related_work.difference: MEMO creates virtual batches through augmentation but uses FIXED augmentations and entropy minimization. We use a LEARNED augmentation policy based on prototype similarity and prototype-guided consistency instead of entropy.

related_work.paper: Dual Prototype Evolving for Test-Time Generalization of Vision-Language Models (DPE)
related_work.difference: DPE targets vision-language models (CLIP) with dual textual-visual prototypes. We target pure vision models with learned augmentation policies. DPE uses fixed augmentations; we learn corruption-specific policies.

related_work.paper: Adaptive-TTA: accuracy-consistent weighted test time augmentation method
related_work.difference: Adaptive-TTA learns augmentation weights for CALIBRATION (Brier score, ECE), not accuracy. It explicitly PRESERVES original predictions—trading accuracy for calibration. It uses fixed policies with scalar weights and does not use prototypes or adapt model parameters. APAC-TTA learns prototype-aware policies for ACCURACY, uses source prototypes for guidance, and adapts BatchNorm parameters via confidence-weighted consistency.

related_work.paper: Improving Test-Time Adaptation via Shift-agnostic Weight Regularization and Nearest Source Prototypes (SWR+NSP)
related_work.difference: SWR+NSP requires source data access BEFORE deployment (offline) for prototype generation but NOT at test time. It uses fixed augmentations and uniform prototype weighting. We only use compact prototypes (no source data at test time), learn augmentation policies, and weight prototypes by confidence. Performance comparison on accuracy metrics is fair since both use similar test-time constraints.

related_work.paper: Test-time classifier adjustment module for model-agnostic domain generalization (T3A)
related_work.difference: T3A adapts classifiers using online feature means with equal prototype weighting. We use pre-computed source prototypes with confidence weighting and learned augmentation policies.

related_work.paper: PROGRAM: Prototype Graph Model based Pseudo-Label Learning for Test-Time Adaptation
related_work.difference: PROGRAM focuses on pseudo-label denoising via prototype graphs and message passing. We focus on learnable augmentation selection and confidence-weighted consistency.

related_work.paper: Test-Time Prompt Tuning for Zero-Shot Generalization in Vision-Language Models (TPT)
related_work.difference: TPT performs prompt tuning for CLIP using simple augmentations. We adapt model parameters for pure vision models with learned corruption-aware augmentations.

CPA uses fixed pseudo-labeling with equal prototype weighting and pre-trained weights as prototypes. We use learned augmentation policies and confidence-weighted prototypes with dedicated prototype computation.

related_work.paper: Decoupled Prototype Learning for Reliable Test-Time Adaptation (DPL)
related_work.difference: DPL uses contrastive prototype learning with fixed augmentations to handle noisy pseudo-labels. We focus on learnable augmentation policies based on prototype similarity and confidence-weighted source prototypes. DPL is primarily batch-based; we target single-image TTA. Our work builds on DPL's prototype-centric insight but addresses augmentation selection—DPL does not learn which augmentations to apply.

hypothesis: Adaptive selection of augmentations based on prototype similarity, combined with confidence-weighted consistency to source prototypes, provides more robust and effective single-image test-time adaptation than fixed augmentation strategies, heuristic augmentation selection, or entropy-based methods, particularly under severe and mixed corruptions.

success_criteria.statistical_significance: p < 0.05 (paired t-test), mean ± SE over 5 independent runs
confirm: APAC-TTA achieves statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) in mCE over MEMO on CIFAR-10-C severity 5 with effect size > 2% absolute improvement
APAC-TTA achieves statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) over Heuristic-APN by >1% mCE (justifying learned policy complexity)
APAC-TTA achieves statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) over SWR+NSP by >1.5% mCE (fair comparison; both use source prototypes but SWR+NSP requires additional offline source data access)
APAC-TTA successfully scales to ImageNet-C with consistent improvements over MEMO baseline
refute: APAC-TTA performs worse than simple MEMO baseline with statistical significance
APAC-TTA shows no improvement over Heuristic-APN (learned policy unnecessary)
Learned augmentation policy provides no benefit over random selection

baselines: SWR+NSP (Choi et al., 2022) - fair comparison; requires offline source data access
DPL (Wang et al., 2024) - recent prototype-based method
DPE (Zhang et al., NeurIPS 2024) - VLM comparison only

compute_requirements.datasets: CIFAR-10/100-C, CIFAR-10.1, ImageNet-C, ImageNet-V2, pre-trained ResNet-50 and WideResNet-28

contingency_plans: If Meta-APN fails: Fallback to heuristic policy using prototype distance for augmentation severity selection
If Meta-APN fails: Use fixed corruption-specific policies based on feature statistics
If Meta-APN fails: Proceed with fixed augmentations + confidence-weighted consistency only
If Gumbel-Softmax unstable: Switch to REINFORCE policy gradient
If confidence weighting degrades: Try alternative confidence metrics (prediction margin, entropy of similarity)
If time-constrained: Reduce scope to CIFAR-10-C only, drop CIFAR-100-C
novelty_claim: This method introduces: (1) a meta-learned augmentation policy network for test-time adaptation that selects augmentations based on prototype similarity, using Gumbel-Softmax for differentiable discrete selection and REINFORCE fallback; (2) confidence-weighted prototype guidance where prototypes are weighted by their consistency across augmented views; (3) a Heuristic-APN baseline (distance-based severity selection) to rigorously justify the complexity of learned policies; and (4) corruption-specific adaptation strategies for single-image TTA on pure vision models. Unlike DPE (VLMs only), MEMO (fixed augmentations + entropy), Adaptive-TTA (calibration focus, no prototypes, preserves predictions), SWR+NSP (requires offline source data, fixed augmentations), and DPL (contrastive learning with fixed augmentations), our method combines learnable policies with confidence-weighted prototypes for standard vision models without source data at test time.

discrete_selection_methods: Gumbel-Softmax with temperature annealing for operation selection
Straight-Through Estimator (STE) for number of augmentations
REINFORCE with baseline as fallback if Gumbel-Softmax unstable
meta_apn_optimization.proxy_objective: Maximize prediction confidence on correct class after augmentation (differentiable surrogate for accuracy)
meta_apn_optimization.continuous_components: Severity scales predicted as continuous values via interpolation

heuristic_baseline.name: Heuristic-APN
heuristic_baseline.description: Simple distance-based severity selection without learned parameters
heuristic_baseline.mechanism: severity = max_severity * (1 - exp(-λ * d)) where d is prototype distance
heuristic_baseline.purpose: Justify complexity of Meta-APN by comparing against simple heuristic

dpl_acknowledgment: DPL (Wang et al., 2024) makes significant contributions to prototype-centric learning for TTA. Our work builds on the insight that prototypes provide robust guidance but addresses a different problem: augmentation selection. DPL uses fixed augmentations for consistency regularization; we learn adaptive policies based on prototype similarity.
adaptive_tta_distinction: Adaptive-TTA (Conde & Premebida, BMVC 2022) explicitly focuses on calibration (Brier score, ECE), not accuracy. It guarantees the predicted class remains unchanged from the original model—trading accuracy for calibration. APAC-TTA targets accuracy improvement and allows class predictions to change when augmented views support a different class. Adaptive-TTA learns scalar weights for fixed-policy aggregation; APAC-TTA learns dynamic augmentation parameters based on prototype similarity.
imagenet_c_inclusion: Full ImageNet-C evaluation included (not just subset) with 5 random seeds
